html:
Multiple cameras: translation sequence
Now five cameras are shown, evenly spaced along the same axis, each with a forward arrow.
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: five identical pinhole camera frustums arranged in a row horizontally (front face toward the viewer)
large red arrows pointing forward along the optical axis in front of each camera
black camera center point at the pinhole of each frustum
optical axis (implicit through each frustum and matching arrow direction)
suggestion of virtual image plane (can be shown as a transparent square per frustum)
background is plain/white (no scene objects, no depth cues)
five-fold repetition highlights concept of continuous forward movement

interactions.label: Number of cameras
interactions.teaches: Demonstrates the effect of showing a sequence of camera positions to illustrate the idea of continuous forward translation

interactions.label: Arrow length
interactions.teaches: Shortening or lengthening the arrows visually encodes the speed of forward camera motion along the optical axis

interactions.label: Camera separation
interactions.teaches: Shows how increasing or decreasing the spacing between cameras helps clarify the trajectory and possible overlap between camera volumes

demo_steps.title: Single camera setup
demo_steps.narration: Here we see a single pinhole camera frustum with a red arrow indicating forward motion along the optical axis. This represents the camera about to move straight toward the scene in front of it.

demo_steps.title: Multiple cameras: translation sequence
demo_steps.narration: Now five cameras are shown, evenly spaced along the same axis, each with a forward arrow. This sequence illustrates the continuous forward translation of the camera: at each moment, the camera occupies one of these positions.

demo_steps.title: Faster forward motion
demo_steps.narration: Increase the arrow length to depict a faster translation speed. The arrows become longer, visually emphasizing the velocity of the camera as it moves forward along its axis.

demo_steps.title: Dense or sparse sampling
demo_steps.narration: Adjust the separation between the cameras. A smaller gap makes the frustums nearly overlap, suggesting more frequent sampling of the forward path; a larger gap makes each intermediate camera position more distinct.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Centered, orthographic view looking directly at the row of camera frustums from slightly above and to the right. All frustums and arrows are clearly visible, with spacing to avoid overlap.
interactionPlan.notes: Render camera frustums as semi-transparent pyramid-shaped meshes with bold black outlines. Arrows should be thick and bright red, always originating at the pinhole and aligned with each frustum's optical axis. Keep the scene minimal—no 3D objects or walls—so the focus stays on the cameras and motion direction.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The red arrows in the generated figure are shorter and thinner than those in the source image.
The frustum geometries are somewhat more 3D-shaped compared to the 2D representations in the source.
The generated figure does not show the large over-extended arrows and spacing typical in the original's composition.
Arrowheads in the generated figure are significantly smaller than in the source image.
There are no background white spaces or black separating lines in the generated version.
evaluation.notes: Highly interactive with all main elements and pedagogical controls; some visual proportion mismatches but concept and usability are excellent.
action_items: Increase red arrow shaft and head thickness/length to more closely match the original figure.
Adjust camera/frustum spacing and sizing so that the overall proportions and layout are closer to the wide, linear spacing of the source figure.
Fine-tune frustum outline thickness to more accurately resemble the heavy lines in the original.
Consider making the arrows overlap or touch the frustum as seen in the original image for clearer propagation.
Review the camera viewpoint/orthogonal projection to better match the flat, head-on view in the source image.

feedback.rationale: All issues are related to visual proportion and layout mismatches, not conceptual errors or missing elements, so improvements are needed in the rendering details.
action_items: Increase red arrow shaft and head thickness/length to more closely match the original figure.

Consider making the arrows overlap or touch the frustum as seen in the original image for clearer propagation.
Review the camera viewpoint/orthogonal projection to better match the flat, head-on view in the source image.

attempts.html:
Multiple cameras: translation sequence
Five evenly spaced pinhole cameras illustrate continuous forward translation.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Frustum side edges in the generated figure are rounded tubes, source uses thin lines.
Arrows in generated figure have different proportions, especially the arrowhead size and base width compared to source.
The camera frustum body and image plane are shaded/translucent gray, whereas the source figure uses only black outlines.
The black dot (aperture) in the generated is a full sphere, in source it is a flat, filled disk.
There is additional UI and interactivity cluttering the view (not present in source).
evaluation.notes: Figure accurately illustrates the translation sequence with excellent interaction, though visual style differs from the 2D source with unnecessary 3D and shading.
action_items: Redraw frustum edges as thin black lines rather than thick black cylinders for closer visual match.
Flatten the black aperture/dot to a 2D disk instead of a 3D sphere.
Remove (or make optional) the translucent fills on frustum and image plane surfaces to match the source line-only style.
Adjust arrow shape to better match the arrowhead style and width ratio in source.
Fine-tune spatial relations to ensure spacing aligns visually with the source and reduce unintended 3D perspective artifacts.

feedback.rationale: All action items concern rendering/styling choices and not the underlying concept or interaction plan, so only the generator needs refinement.
action_items: Redraw frustum edges as thin black lines rather than thick black cylinders for closer visual match.
Flatten the black aperture/dot to a 2D disk instead of a 3D sphere.

Adjust arrow shape to better match the arrowhead style and width ratio in source.
Fine-tune spatial relations to ensure spacing aligns visually with the source and reduce unintended 3D perspective artifacts.

attempts.html:
Multiple cameras: translation sequence
Now five cameras are shown, evenly spaced along the same axis, each with a forward arrow.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The 3D camera frustum is visually thicker and more rounded than the sharp 2D shapes in the original.
The frustum's image plane is darker and not as sharply outlined as in the source.
The red arrows are a close match but have slightly rounder/softer tips and bases in 3D.
There is no black border on the image plane in the generated version.
UI controls and explanatory labels are present in the generated figure (not in source).
evaluation.notes: The core translation-sequence concept is preserved and all main elements are present with exceptionally strong interactivity and labeling, though minor 3D stylization makes the frustum geometry softer than the sharp-edged 2D drawings.
action_items: Refine frustum rendering to use sharper edges and thinner outlines, more closely matching the style of the 2D source.
Make the image plane's border stronger and more rectangular, with higher contrast gray/black lines, not just soft mesh shading.
Reduce the 3D roundness in the red arrow's tip and tail, making it a flatter 2D-style arrow.
Add an option to hide or show the UI for presentations.
Tweak the proportions to make frustum bases more slender, if possible, to match the original better.

feedback.rationale: All core concepts and elements are correct, but visual rendering and detail adjustments are needed to match the original 2D style more faithfully.
action_items: Refine frustum rendering to use sharper edges and thinner outlines, more closely matching the style of the 2D source.
Make the image plane's border stronger and more rectangular, with higher contrast gray/black lines, not just soft mesh shading.
Reduce the 3D roundness in the red arrow's tip and tail, making it a flatter 2D-style arrow.
Add an option to hide or show the UI for presentations.
Tweak the proportions to make frustum bases more slender, if possible, to match the original better.
